question: Rainbow Six Siege: FURIA Esports vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO1) - South America League Kickoff Group B
description:
This market refers to the Rainbow Six Siege match between FURIA Esports and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the South America League Kickoff Group B, initially scheduled for March 31 at 8:45PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA Esports" if FURIA Esports win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against FURIA Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: FURIA Esports enters as the heavy 75.5% trader favorite against Ninjas in Pyjamas in this BO1 South America League Kickoff Group B opener, driven by a dominant 6-1 head-to-head record—including 3-1 over the last 12 months with a 5-2 map edge—and superior recent form boasting a 70% win rate overall versus NiP's 50%. FURIA's fresh roster overhaul eight days ago, featuring high-profile additions like ex-G2's Loira, Team Liquid's DiasLucasBr, Fluxo W7M's volpz, and in-game leader HerdsZ, signals upgrades from their Six Invitational 2026 campaign, while NiP's lineup with new captain Bassetto shows continuity amid a 20% win rate in the past 30 days. The wisdom of crowds prices in FURIA's stylistic matchup advantages in this high-stakes group stage decider.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fluxo W7M leads trader consensus at 77.5% implied probability for this BO1 group stage clash in South America League 2026 Kickoff Group B, propelled by their momentum from a 4th-place playoffs run at Six Invitational 2026, where star player Paluh earned top global ratings with 1.13 average and EVP honors. LOUD, meanwhile, faltered in recent international qualifiers without matching that elite form, despite a narrow past BO1 head-to-head win over W7M precursors in 2025. Fluxo's stable roster, bolstered by kondz's March 21 addition, contrasts LOUD's six-man lineup amid round-robin volatility, underscoring the wisdom of crowds on superior recent tournament results and matchup dynamics.
